name: Qasim ibn Hasan
title: Memory of Hasan
shortDescription: The 13-year-old son of Imam Hasan (AS) who considered death in the way of God sweeter than honey.
fullStory:
Qasim ibn al-Hasan (AS) was the young son of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) and the brave grandson of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). After the martyrdom of his noble father, he was raised under the care of his uncle, Imam Hussain (AS). Imam Hussain's love for Qasim was so profound that he treated him like his own sons, and Qasim saw his uncle as a father figure.

On the night of Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) announced the impending martyrdom of all his companions, Qasim, who was only 13 or 14 years old, anxiously asked, 'Will I also be killed tomorrow?' To test him, the Imam asked, 'How do you view death?' Qasim, with unparalleled firmness reflecting his lofty Alawi spirit, replied, 'Ahla min al-Asal' (Sweeter than honey). This historical phrase eternalized the high level of knowledge and certainty of this youth in history.

According to the report in Sheikh Mufid's Al-Irshad, on the day of Ashura, after the martyrdom of the companions, Qasim came to Imam Hussain (AS) asking for permission to fight. The Imam, unable to bear seeing the bloody body of his brother's memory, initially refused. Qasim wept and kissed his uncle's hands and feet so much that finally, with tearful eyes, the Imam granted him permission.

Sheikh Mufid writes in Al-Irshad: Qasim entered the battlefield with a face shining like a piece of the moon. Despite his young age, he fought remarkably. In the midst of the battle, his sandal strap broke. As he bent down to fix it (confident that the enemy would not dare attack him), Amr ibn Sa'd ibn Nufayl al-Azdi seized the opportunity and struck a heavy sword blow to his head. Qasim fell to the ground and cried out, 'O uncle, come to my aid'.

Imam Hussain (AS) rushed to Qasim like a hunting falcon and killed his murderer. When the dust of battle settled, the Imam sat by Qasim's side as the youth was rubbing his feet on the ground in intense pain. With a broken heart, the Imam said, 'How hard it is for your uncle that you call him but he cannot help you, or his help is of no use to you'. The Imam then embraced Qasim's lifeless body and laid it next to the body of Ali Akbar.
quote: Death in the way of God is sweeter than honey to me.
